messages.content: Hi. A few updates and one serious issue I want to raise.

Hi Priya — you're about 3 months in now. I have the full history here. Before you go into the new issue, just to close the loop: the Slack intermittent bug — did the engineering team follow up within 3 business days as promised?

Yes actually — they did follow up, and they pushed a targeted fix specifically for our workspace configuration about 2 weeks ago. It's been completely stable since then. No more triple notifications. That was handled well.

Really glad to hear that — and good to have the Slack issue formally resolved after 7 weeks. I'm updating the status on your account: Slack integration bug, opened Day 18, intermittent recurrence confirmed Day 45 and Day 67, targeted workspace fix deployed approximately Day 75, fully resolved as of now. Closed. What's the new issue?

So we did hire those 3 people like I said we would. We're now at 13 team members. Everything is fine on the seat side. But here's the serious issue — we just lost a major client. It was our biggest retainer, about 40% of our revenue. So we're going through a difficult period and I need to have an honest conversation about our FlowDesk subscription.

I'm sorry to hear that — losing a client of that size is genuinely difficult and I want to approach this conversation honestly rather than just defensively. I can see your full account history here: you're 3 months in on Pro Annual, you've had a discount of 15% locked in at renewal, you've flagged budget pressure before, and the client portal Gantt gap is still outstanding. Tell me what you're thinking — are you looking at suspending the account, downgrading, or something else? I want to understand what would actually help your business right now.

I need to reduce costs in the short term. But I also genuinely like FlowDesk and my team is now deeply embedded in it — migrating everything somewhere else would be a nightmare. What options do I have?

Given your history and the circumstances, here's what I can offer honestly. The 15% renewal discount is already locked in — that's confirmed regardless of this conversation. Additionally, given the documented feature gap with client portal Gantt and the Slack issues you endured over 7 weeks, I can escalate to our retention team for a temporary account credit to offset some of the period where the product wasn't fully delivering for you. I also want to be transparent: we have a "pause" option that's not publicly advertised — you can pause your account for up to 60 days, which freezes billing while preserving all your data and team configurations. That buys you some breathing room if the next 2 months are the crunch period. Would a 60-day pause be useful, or is the ongoing tooling more important to keep active?
The 60-day pause is interesting but my team actively uses FlowDesk every day. I can't pause it while they're in the middle of client work. What about the credit you mentioned?

Completely fair — pausing while actively using it makes no sense. Let me be specific about the credit: given the Slack integration bug that persisted intermittently for approximately 7 weeks, and the client portal feature gap that was presented as available but turned out to be limited at the time of your signup, I'm submitting a formal credit request to our retention team for the equivalent of 2 months of your Pro plan — that's approximately $50 credit applied to your account immediately, which reduces your effective spend. Combined with the 15% renewal discount already locked in, this is our concrete acknowledgment of the gaps in your experience. I can't make the client portal Gantt appear faster but I can make the cost of waiting for it more reasonable.
That's fair. The $50 credit and the 15% renewal discount together make this a much easier decision to stay. Let's do that.

Done — $50 credit applied to your account effective today, and the 15% renewal discount remains locked in. I'm updating your account status: churn risk flagged Day 89 due to client revenue loss, resolved with $50 service credit and confirmed 15% renewal discount. Account retained. I also want to make a note that you now have 13 team members, your team is deeply embedded in the platform, and the primary outstanding feature gap is client portal Gantt, which CS has told you is 6 months out from Day 67 — so we're probably 4 months away now. I'll flag your account for a proactive CS check-in at the 6-month mark to share an update on that roadmap item. Is there anything else I can help you with today?
One last thing — I want to add my co-founder to the account as a second admin. Her name is Riya Desai. Can you help with that?

Absolutely — to add Riya as an admin, go to Settings > Team > Members, find her name (or invite her by email if she's not yet on the account), click the role dropdown next to her name, and select "Admin." She'll have the same permissions as you including billing visibility and add-on approvals. Given the unauthorized add-on purchase incident from Day 18, I'd recommend having a quick conversation with her about the approval workflow that's now in place — any add-on purchase requests will come to both of you for approval once she's an admin. I've noted Riya Desai added as second admin on your account.
Perfect. Thank you — you've been genuinely helpful today.

I appreciate that — and I want to acknowledge that your journey with us hasn't been smooth. Provisioning issues on Day 1, a billing surprise, a 7-week integration bug, a feature gap that affected your core use case, and now external business pressure. You've been patient and direct throughout, and that's made it possible to actually help you. I hope the next 3 months are much quieter. Take care, Priya.
